content.subtitle: Some verbs followed by -ing
items: admit, avoid, deny, discuss, dislike, enjoy, feel like, finish, give up, mention, mind, practise, suggest, take up

content.subtitle: Useful hints
items: After a preposition, we usually use -ing: I'm afraid of flying.
Phrases ending in to (as a preposition) are also followed by -ing: I look forward to hearing from you.
We can use the -ing form as the subject of a sentence: Cooking is great fun!

content.subtitle: Some verbs followed by full infinitive (to + verb)
items: advise, afford, agree, choose, decide, expect, help, hope, invite, learn, manage, offer, plan, pretend, promise, refuse, seem, teach, tell, want, would like

content.subtitle: Watch out!
items: Some verbs are usually followed by an object + bare infinitive (without to): let, make.
In the passive, make is followed by full infinitive: Michael was made to apologise by his mother.
Some verbs can be followed by the infinitive alone or by object + full infinitive: He wants me to tell him a joke.

content.subtitle: Same or nearly same meaning
items: With begin, continue, hate, like, love, prefer and start, we can often use either -ing or infinitive.

content.subtitle: Meaning changes
rows: have a memory in your mind
do something you were planning to do
not be able to remember a past event
not do something you were planning to do
interrupt an action to do something else
do something to solve a problem
make an effort to do something

sections.title: A: -ing or infinitive
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I really enjoy ...... (read) in the evening.
They decided ...... (move) to a bigger flat.
We managed ...... (finish) on time.
She avoids ...... (eat) fast food.
Do you mind ...... (open) the window?
He promised ...... (help) me tomorrow.

sections.title: B: After prepositions
sections.instructions: Write the -ing form after each preposition.

items.sentence: I'm interested in ...... (learn) Japanese.
Thanks for ...... (help) me with my homework.
She's good at ...... (draw).
He's afraid of ...... (fly).
We talked about ...... (go) away for the weekend.
I look forward to ...... (see) you.

sections.title: C: Meaning changes
sections.instructions: Choose the correct form to complete each sentence.

items.sentence: I remembered ...... the door, so we didn't need to go back. **locking / to lock**
Remember ...... your umbrella — it might rain. **taking / to take**
I stopped ...... because I had a sore throat. **talking / to talk**
We stopped ...... some water on our way home. **buying / to buy**
Try ...... this app if your phone is slow. **using / to use**
I'm trying ...... less sugar this month. **eating / to eat**

sections.title: D: Bare infinitive
sections.instructions: Complete with the correct infinitive form.

items.sentence: My parents let me ...... (stay) out late.
The teacher made us ...... (rewrite) the paragraph.
I was made ...... (apologise) to my brother.
Her coach helped her ...... (improve) her serve.
They told us ...... (wait) outside.
Would you like ...... (join) us for dinner?
He suggested ...... by train.
I can't afford ...... a new laptop right now.
Do you feel like ...... out tonight?
Please don't forget ...... me when you arrive.
She made me ...... the truth.
Cooking at home helps us ...... money.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
I have a memory of seeing that film. **REMEMBER**
I ...... that film.

Don't forget that you must send the email. **REMEMBER**
...... the email.

We interrupted our walk in order to have a coffee. **STOPPED**
We ...... a coffee.

I attempted to solve it by restarting the computer. **TRY**
...... the computer.

They caused us to wait for an hour. **MADE**
They ...... for an hour.

In passive form, we use to after make. **MADE**
He ...... apologise.
